required_sections.notes: §3 LOs ~75w ✓. §4 Introduction ~320w ✓. §5 Core Concepts ~1540w across 5 subsections ✓. §10 Key Takeaways ~180w ✓. All required sections present and non-trivial.

citation_density.notes: All 4 primary resources cited at least once. R1 (Databricks): §4, §5.1, §5.2 steps 2/5, §5.3, §7, §8.1. R2 (DEV Community): §4, §5.2, §5.3, §5.4, §6, §7, §8.3, §8.5. R3 (Prompt Engineering Guide): §5.1, §5.2 step 5, §5.3, §6, §7, §8.1. R4 (Machine Learning Plus): §5.2 steps 1/4/5, §5.5, §6, §7, §8.2, §8.5. ≥85% claim citation rate in §5/§6/§7.

sequence_drift.notes: No forward references. All terms defined in this corpus or prior topics. Embeddings, vectors, ANN, HNSW, cosine similarity, top-k, metadata, vector database all from 14.1. Chunking strategy deferred to Semester 2. Hallucination causation deferred to 14.3. When-to-use-RAG deferred to 14.4.

length_sanity.notes: ~3,600w — within standard range. Cycle 1 failed at ~1,965w; revision added WHY+failure-mode per pipeline step, prompt-template 3-element analysis, index/query phase latency detail, 5-item failure-point checklist in §6, 5th real-world use case (medical Q&A), and §8.5 (similarity threshold best practice).

scope_creep.notes: Scope maintained on RAG pipeline mechanics. Hallucination causation → 14.3. When to use → 14.4. Chunking strategy → Semester 2. All correctly named-and-deferred.

beginner_readability.notes: Terms defined on first use. Friend-with-manual analogy in §4 accessible. Refund-terms query example traces all 5 steps concretely. WHY+failure-mode per step scaffolds understanding. Code block (standard vs RAG prompt) concrete and annotated. No jargon dumps.

soft_findings: §8.2 (Choose top-k) at ~90w is the shortest subsection — a concrete k=3 vs k=10 example would strengthen it, but current length meets minimum bar.
§9 Exercise is observation-only — an optional trace-the-pipeline micro-task could increase active recall, non-blocking.
verdict: PASS (cycle 2) — all 6 checks green. Corpus correctly scoped to 5-step RAG retrieval pipeline. All 4 primary sources cited with high claim-citation density. No forward references. ~3,600w within standard range. Beginner readability maintained via analogy, concrete examples, and per-step failure-mode scaffolding.
